questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The $\alpha$-decay of Po-210 nuclei (in ground state at rest) is accompanied by emission of two groups of alpha particles with kinetic energies 4.706 MeV and 4.50 MeV . Following the emission of these particles, the daughter nuclei are found in the ground state and excited states. Find energy of gamma photon emitted by excited nuclei. Give answer in KeV . Make the approximation,
$\frac{\text { Massof daughter nuclide }}{\text { Massof } \alpha \text {-particle }}=\frac{\text { Massnumber of daughter nuclide }}{\text { Massnumber of Henuclide }}$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Po}^{210} \rightarrow \mathrm{X}^*+\alpha \longrightarrow \mathrm{X}+\alpha$, where $\mathrm{X}^*$ and X are excited daughter nuclide formed and daughter nuclide in
ground state. $\mathrm{X}^{\star} \rightarrow \mathrm{X}+\gamma$, energy of $\gamma$-photon $=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{x}}{ }^{\star}-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{x}}=\left(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{K}_\alpha\right)-\left(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{x}}{ }^{\prime}+\mathrm{K}_\alpha{ }^{\prime}\right)=\frac{210}{206}\left(\mathrm{~K}_\alpha-\mathrm{K}_\alpha{ }^{\prime}\right)$
$\left[\frac{\mathrm{Kx}}{\mathrm{K} \alpha}=\frac{4}{206}=\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{x}}^{\prime}}{\mathrm{K}_\alpha^{\prime}}\right]=210 \mathrm{KeV}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Binding energy per nucleon versus mass number curve for nuclei is shown in figure. $A, B, C$ and $D$ are four nuclei indicated on the curve. The $Q$ value of following nuclear reaction $A+B \rightarrow C$ is $N \times 10^7 \mathrm{eV}$. Find the value of $N$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A neutron with kinetic energy $K=10 \mathrm{MeV}$ activates a nuclear reaction $n+{ }^{12} \mathrm{C} \rightarrow{ }^9 \mathrm{Be}+\alpha$ whose threshold $E_{\text {th }}$ $=6.17 \mathrm{MeV}$. Find the kinetic energy of the alpha particles outgoing at right angles to the incoming neutron's direction(mark the answer in $\mathrm{MeV}$). [Take $1 \mathrm{amu}=931.1 \mathrm{MeV}$ ]
questions.solution: $n+c^{12} \longrightarrow B e^9+\alpha$ Let $p$ be the momentum of neutron $\begin{aligned} & \frac{p^2}{2 m_n}=\frac{p_2^2}{2 m_\alpha}+\frac{p_1^2}{2 m_{B e}}+Q \\ & p_1 \cos \theta=p, \quad p_1 \sin \theta=p_2 \\ & \frac{p^2}{2 m_n}=\frac{p^2}{2 m_{B e}}+\frac{p_2^2}{2 m_{B e}}+\frac{p_2^2}{2 m_\alpha}+Q \\ & \begin{aligned} & {[10-10 / 9]=K_\alpha+K_\alpha \times \frac{4}{9}+Q } \\ &= \frac{13 K_\alpha}{9}+Q \\ & Q=6.17 \times \frac{12}{13} \end{aligned} \\ & K_\alpha=2.21 \mathrm{MeV} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In certain experiment it has been found that the ratio of the decay current in a L-R circuit to the activity of a radioactive sample remains constant with time. The time constant of $L-R$ circuit is 3 sec , find the average life (in second) of radioactive sample.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{A}=\mathrm{A}_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda \mathrm{t}}$ and $\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{I}_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{t} / \lambda} ; \frac{I_0}{A_0} e^{\left(-\frac{1}{\tau}+\lambda\right) t}=\frac{i}{A}$
for constant value of $\frac{i}{A}, \lambda-\frac{1}{\tau}=0 \quad \therefore \lambda=\frac{1}{\tau} ; \mathrm{T}_{\text {mean }}=3 \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a slow reaction, heat is being evolved at a rate about 10 mW in a liquid. If the heat were being generated by the decay of ${ }^{32} \mathrm{P}$, a radioactive isotope of phosphorus that has half-life of 14 days and emits only beta-particles with a mean energy of 700 KeV , estimate the number of ${ }^{32} \mathrm{P}$ atoms in the liquid. Express your answer in form of $\mathrm{A} \times 10^{17}$ and fill 5 A in OMR sheet. [Take $: \ln 2=0.675$ ]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A radioactive element, which decays by emission of $\alpha$-particles, has a mean lifetime of $10^5 \mathrm{~s}$. A battery is constructed by taking $10^{-9}$ mole of the element in the form of its oxide and causing the emitted $\alpha$-particles to be deposited on an anode, giving an electric current. $50 \%$ of the $\alpha$-particles are captured after the decay, leading to a current. This current is passed through a $1 \mathrm{M} \Omega$ resistance, and the potential drop $(\mathrm{V})$ across it is measured. Find the time, in minutes, after which V equals 0.48 mV . [Take In $2=0.69$ Avogadro's No., $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6$ $\left.\times 10^{23}\right]$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & N=N_0 e^{-\lambda t} \\ & -\frac{d N}{d t}=\lambda N_0 e^{-\lambda t}\end{aligned}$
$I=2 e\left(-\frac{d N}{d t}\right) \times \eta$
$V=I R$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
